name: Reverend Warnock
subject: I want to hear from you, friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
I'm ready to flip the great state of Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats - but I can't do it without you.
| | |
---
Friend,

_We are in a fight for the soul of our nation 's democracy._
As the senior pastor at Ebenezer Baptist Church, the spiritual home of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., I think about Dr. King's words often - especially his urgent call for a "revolution of values." And lately, with politicians like Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell in office, these words ring truer than ever.
Corrupt leaders have silenced millions with systematic voter suppression. They've attacked our health care. They've abused their power, and they've led an all-out assault on the character of our country.
If we don't respond in this moment of crisis with the moral leadership that these issues deserve, what will our country become?
**Friend, that 's why I'm running for U.S. Senate - to restore moral leadership to our government and make sure every single person has a voice in our democracy. ****But I won 't be able to flip Georgia blue without hearing what's important to people like you.** [**So I need your help: Will you please take just a few moments to take our April strategy survey? Your feedback is essential to making sure we flip this seat and take back the Senate for Democrats.**](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk134-su-nd")
I've dedicated myself to my community and worked hard to help others realize their fullest potential - because I know what it's like to not be born with a seat at the table. We have to make the system work for everyone, and it's exactly what I plan to do in the Senate.
I'm ready to make health care affordable and accessible for all - because one bad diagnosis shouldn't send our families spiraling. Ready to fight for the dignity of workers. Ready to get out the vote, not suppress the vote. Ready to stand up for the kid growing up in the projects and communities across Georgia, who, like me, wants to achieve _their_ American dream.
**I 'm ready to flip the great state of Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats - but I can't do it without you.**
**[Friend, I need your input as soon as possible. Please take our brief survey to help inform our strategy for flipping this seat and taking back the Senate for Democrats.](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk134-su-nd")**
| | [Take the Survey](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-strategy?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk134-su-nd")
---
Thanks for sharing your feedback. I'm looking forward to reading your responses.

Reverend Warnock
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e680/288179be/ad09914/348efaaa/2215925144/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") | | (C) 2020 | Paid for by Warnock for Georgia.

name: =?utf-8?B?VGVhbSBTw6FuY2hleg==?=
subject: Every voter deserves...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez
body:
_**The option to vote by mail. **_

Not only are mail-in elections associated with higher voter participation, a universal Vote by Mail option would also prevent the further spread of coronavirus this November by allowing voters to stay home.
**That 's why Congresswoman Sanchez is demanding voters in all 50 states have the option to Vote by Mail. [Will you stand with her by signing on?](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/FVxobj5T4Kkvq-aspGP3SfX0-nE2ZvVKN6xgtgsTGKs=)**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/FVxobj5T4Kkvq-aspGP3SfX0-nE2ZvVKN6xgtgsTGKs=)
Even if our ongoing health crisis has subsided by November, it's critical we prepare for _all_ possible realities well in advance. That's to say, if the coronavirus crisis _does_ continue through fall, we need to start prepping for a mail-in election _now_.
Unfortunately, there are millions of Americans who do not currently have the option to Vote by Mail. We _must_ take federal action to protect the safety and well-being of voters, poll workers, and election officials.

**[Demand ALL Americans have the option to Vote by Mail | SIGN YOUR NAME>>](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/FVxobj5T4Kkvq-aspGP3SfX0-nE2ZvVKN6xgtgsTGKs=)**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/FVxobj5T4Kkvq-aspGP3SfX0-nE2ZvVKN6xgtgsTGKs=)
Thank you for taking action,
\-- _Team S anchez_
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/j_f7Z86Qs9Cm4jSSuB2iFrvZOEXOjLlHp8hYm3HDo3w=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/o-i6CDyAW0IkSimI62T3mDddr2L9vkUOFfTXMMpq64c=)
**For more information about COVID-19, including official updates, please visit _[https://www.cdc.gov/](https://act.myngp.com/el/D4NM9oRtZpGFeTDgR_yeP7nBHBM_accGpmUJ5rhDwr4=/1VvZOauQV7ev8TUWFWm1ke1mQTcARDD8f0Hfiy22Wkk=)_**

name: Dean Browning
subject: We can all help in the ongoing effort to combat COVID-19
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Dear Patriot,
We can all help in the ongoing effort to combat COVID-19 and I wanted to pass along information about both local and national organizations you can connect with to support our neighbors and our community as follows: [Allentown Rescue Mission](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RzuTuasSW0yU8_47Oqh2R-yWQwGddXUXYvEtdpsiKKONnuuFQUCJDYghxqQJTyQTpYq8E0YYu0cD04tcxT7rkltdmPQyN1oHBjjrJMUwehYM=&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) Providing services to the homeless through emergency shelter, food, and other life-sustaining services
[American Red Cross](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2Rdo--LAEsZL1v2uyPqgYE6bB_BnZ5m8LjtE4at0Bv5q0aMQZjFEfXGk0nkWlRmmmLrnqCijytl8ooyaR0x-m_Pg==&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) The Red Cross is in critical need due to the outbreak as blood drives continue to be canceled. Please consider making an appointment to donate lifesaving blood, platelets, and AL Elite plasma.
[Feeding Pennsylvania](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2R2A-YW6A05eWuu6AxXkngmVGF0vLoXFU6oOMQVJtauSNFlJsbqBYwuba-OHSspxfprsP4M6O3zXS5_vpV-IRBvw==&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) Supporting local food banks here in Pennsylvania, distributing food to families hit the hardest by the outbreak. [Miller-Keystone Blood Center](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2R_Um2su7f5ttE3uZlBoUltW0aDCFWqto4tP7FmXeABPzcfNaLAloc7VXV6_pCrLm6sCO20Cluitnrssbp7VXZSw==&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) In addition to the Red Cross, please consider contacting Miller-Keystone to make an appointment to donate blood.
[Pocono Mountains United Way](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RGXzY-Kmau6n9Ox-CjcOZSbx4CxyvoIWRbAXVNxmbmxekxSbGwhXkfIzc10rp8D7eWjqemhj2mzoUwF-ck79fJQ==&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) To support the short and long-term financial recovery of people in Monroe County.
[Second Harvest Food Bank](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RITEIiY7khOUgEA3fa1n5fWbvpjLd28jUckhjhn2v1nGT2Y_s0D3K7FWkJAWDfcRMxEfBPCUmpm8=&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) Help obtain food and distribute it to people in need and those hit the hardest by the outbreak.
[Salvation Army](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RavAHIl119wL40KU4P06LiPCDHO6xF02aPCwR4-61K0u-istk4r3LmdnImKJ7Ru9gHHRbNCwhoi4JwMyQIbi9p9gYiwUeWD3xQCYcHFq6XeVaMvq7yt5pLA==&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) Financial contributions allow them to quickly respond to changing needs. In-kind goods of non-perishable food, baby supplies, hygiene products, cleaning, and sanitizing items, and paper products are also welcome at select Salvation Army facilities.
[United Way of the Greater Lehigh Valley COVID-19 Community Response Fund](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2Rf_kGCj1YekrytnYEr1cU8O09cMiuA2As0l-AWZii9BnJf1-0nGxWoLX0XFICifVxfXdi6sIgYDSOpA6UmwcjqUu5Mm196gNbUQrfwuwEyC6cwD_uo-uqSmgmt-KhI4En&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==)[ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2Rf_kGCj1YekrytnYEr1cU8O09cMiuA2As0l-AWZii9BnJf1-0nGxWoLX0XFICifVxfXdi6sIgYDSOpA6UmwcjqUu5Mm196gNbUQrfwuwEyC6cwD_uo-uqSmgmt-KhI4En&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) The COVID-19 Community Response Fund is a local, coordinated emergency response fund to support the short and long-term financial recovery of people in the Lehigh Valley.
[YMCA - Allentown](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RUzou4cWu6MjVorUKQ6eTVZZiWqu4lljFIy8yduMmj6A_h_iHGCFkCjcE-lIbMrGTgjFTU7DO8wHmbdAKeyrJR1_JbspX-20SwgVGWqImzbQ=&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) or [YMCA - Pocono](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RGiIBq-BCL7G7H4b6FgZtUCnxxyGpYlEx274WMT2wmdUGn_gXJOxWiadRbNsPRNHTdkq5FpuK4dvCsa-ZJb4MBg==&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) Providing emergency child-care for health care workers and first responders; food for children without access to school meals; and outreach to seniors in social isolation. If you're self-isolating or in a high-risk group, donating funds, medical supplies or care packages is a great way to support our community. If you are otherwise healthy, in a low-risk group, and engaging in social distancing, you may also want to consider volunteering as your help is desperately needed. I also continue to encourage you to visit [www.coronavirus.gov](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001RNd-_z_R5i6ePGKqT8lC1OLA6YdvZD5lAXcdTgedouSK1wq4w8OJNqTQYvYa7d2RRKl_q2EEWKUAlV_G0s_vAHkXkPRqgwsFEKM063NXvf7rFV3dhe2ZPKx7Ffd6xpIA45Bhz-I9heUHSwb4ZO2M4dwzp4gOHYiLmowCmX21SfiY4yCiNiwG5QUpuYPya3PoRRwmqEMaaGW-Ffq_oK9q5JPDVWm0zMTcVySwJOKnbxGis0Ie4Dpn3i5ekqpaqTfLRhw2S6Aaqa0DhMvxo34nTMYAlxclikijneCs926w5xm_GLWgDX-dtj-GRU1qK1AxYcH566j_NqgkfIhswAabVT3lqD7_ytB-8kyEbVxYpk1h0KBS2ENBxCIQO2_dl50oLuzSRQXpT7PjYBgrHgFCIUy9F1AbOGVjPwBx99rrCvHjnx6RPXyNyXt8zC1Kde_dA1vOXooJT54eE9vgDoudNa3p2xMb_3PKkfgyj4mMUnOenFxZvlIC7SD4HyodiV_L6RYW_8MUwB0DAC0TiKvqHzzz-wkIviIAZBxyrCnMEEBQQCKH_EjlmCap54luqIIfXyueD1FQLrri9JKwri7y7gX7hIae2gph3Lkjqy3AmoTPxT2TsITePkuoNL_noEqzuuPU9pvVylbecHuL8yAa2Wvpy2kkMv-rJ0cML4Dd1kA=&c=HKV2TcxX0sv_vvGn5c-P38XDGSAIdw8NOlVWEjVOBU0xQgtk09qIuA==&ch=k18f4IiFfzssdQAojLO0MGv6dywCYdn17moC2wi9M7QXd8Ci45Q3jA==) to access helpful tips and ways to keep yourself and your loved ones safe during this outbreak, including:

* Washing your hands
* Cleaning frequently touched objects and surfaces
* Avoiding contact with those who are sick
* Staying home if you are sick

Thank you and stay healthy and safe.

For Freedom,
---
|
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | Dear Patriot, We can all help in the ongoing effort to combat COVID-19 and I wanted to pass along information about both local and national organizations you can connect with to support our neighbors and our community as follows: == Providing services to the homeless through emergency shelter, food, and other life-sustaining services == The Red Cross is in critical need due to the outbreak as blood drives continue to be canceled. Please consider making an appointment to donate lifesaving blood, platelets, and AL Elite plasma. == Supporting local food banks here in Pennsylvania, distributing food to families hit the hardest by the outbreak. == In addition to the Red Cross, please consider contacting Miller-Keystone to make an appointment to donate blood. == To support the short and long-term financial recovery of people in Monroe County. == Help obtain food and distribute it to people in need and those hit the hardest by the outbreak. == Financial contributions allow them to quickly respond to changing needs. In-kind goods of non-perishable food, baby supplies, hygiene products, cleaning, and sanitizing items, and paper products are also welcome at select Salvation Army facilities. == The COVID-19 Community Response Fund is a local, coordinated emergency response fund to support the short and long-term financial recovery of people in the Lehigh Valley. == or == Providing emergency child-care for health care workers and first responders; food for children without access to school meals; and outreach to seniors in social isolation. If you're self-isolating or in a high-risk group, donating funds, medical supplies or care packages is a great way to support our community. If you are otherwise healthy, in a low-risk group, and engaging in social distancing, you may also want to consider volunteering as your help is desperately needed. I also continue to encourage you to visit == to access helpful tips and ways to keep yourself and your loved ones safe during this outbreak, including: Washing your hands Cleaning frequently touched objects and surfaces Avoiding contact with those who are sick Staying home if you are sick Thank you and stay healthy and safe. For Freedom, | | Dean Browning Republican for Congress, PA-07 | | | == | == == | | | Dean Browning | 2432 W. Congress Street, Allentown, PA 18104 | | Sent by mailto:dean@browningforcongress.com in collaboration with |

name: Wesley Hunt
subject: Coronavirus Resources
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

There is no question that these past few weeks have been difficult for many Houstonians. The Coronavirus represents an unprecedented challenge to our city, state and country. It has endangered not just the health of our communities, but also the economic reality for many.

For that reason, my team and I put together a list of resources that could prove useful to you or someone you know who is struggling to cope. We hope they help anyone struggling through these difficult times.

**Preventing the spread of the Coronavirus:**
White House 30 Days to Slow the Spread - https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/03.16.20_coronavirus-guidance_8.5x11_315PM.pdf

Center for Disease Control Coronavirus Guidelines - https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/hcp/guidance-prevent-spread.html

Houston Health Department - https://houstonemergency.org/covid19

Harris County - http://publichealth.harriscountytx.gov/Resources/2019-Novel-Coronavirus

**Do I need to see a doctor or be tested?**
Center for Disease Control Self-Checker - https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/symptoms-testing/testing.html

**Unemployment Services**

Texas Workforce Commission - https://www.twc.texas.gov/jobseekers/unemployment-benefits-services

Federal Government Assistance - https://www.usa.gov/unemployment

**Resources for Small Business Owners**

Small Business Administration Program Information - https://www.sba.gov/disaster-assistance/coronavirus-covid-19

Houston Small Business Administration Office - https://www.sba.gov/offices/district/tx/houston

Texas Updates on CARES Act - https://gov.texas.gov/uploads/files/business/CARES_Act_and_PPP_Loan_Overview.pdf

US Senate Guide to CARES Act - https://www.sbc.senate.gov/public/_cache/files/9/7/97ac840c-28b7-4e49-b872-d30a995d8dae/F2CF1DD78E6D6C8C8C3BF58C6D1DDB2B.small-business-owner-s-guide-to-the-cares-act-final-.pdf

**Mental Health**

Center for Disease Control - https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/daily-life-coping/managing-stress-anxiety.html

**Access to Food**

Houston Food Bank - https://www.houstonfoodbank.org/covid19/

For Seniors wary of busy grocery stores here’s a list of grocery stores with special senior hours - https://www.khou.com/article/news/health/coronavirus/houston-area-stores-with-special-shopping-hours-for-seniors/285-750e1ab3-e18a-4b0a-9129-777a585e62b5

As Houston always does, I'm confident that we'll get through this together.

Sincerely,
Wesley Hunt

name: Booker HQ
subject: Important information about resources you can use
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CORY BOOKER FOR SENATE
body:
A few things you might have missed from the COVID-19 stimulus package |
#
Friends, Last week, Cory helped negotiate and then voted for the CARES Act -- a nearly $2 trillion stimulus package meant to address the health and economic impacts caused by the coronavirus. It's a critical piece of legislation that will go a long way toward protecting our health and safety while helping families and small businesses across our country stay afloat. **You may have already heard about the direct cash payments and expanded unemployment benefits -- two major components of the relief package. But below is a quick recap of five parts of the coronavirus stimulus bill you might have missed.**
* **Financial support for small businesses.** This bill will extend billions of dollars in aid to support small businesses who keep workers on payroll during this crisis Known as the Payroll Protection Program, the federal government will guarantee forgivable loans for small businesses in the U.S. to cover operating expenses for eight weeks. Operating expenses include utilities, payroll expenses, or mortgage or rent payments.

* **Help for our health care system.** The CARES Act allocates billions of dollars to help hospitals ensure they have the equipment and resources they need to take care of people who need help. Local hospitals, public entities, non-profit entities, and Medicare and Medicaid enrolled suppliers and institutional providers can apply for grants to cover unreimbursed health care related expenses or lost revenue.

* **Investing in our students and educators.** The CARES Act provides funding to K-12 and higher education to help educators and students transition to online and remote learning. HBCUs and other minority-serving institutions will also receive much-needed financial aid.

* **Support for affordable housing.** Homeowners can seek up to a one-year forbearance on all federally-backed mortgages. The bill provides a 60-day moratorium on foreclosures and evictions on all federally-backed mortgages, 90-day forbearance on federally-backed loans for owners of multifamily rental properties, and 120-day eviction protection for tenants.

* **Child care for essential workers.** The bill provides access to high-quality child care for frontline workers like health care sector employees, emergency responders, sanitation workers, and others deemed essential during the response to the coronavirus.

Even with this help on the way, the fight goes on and there's much more work to do. Cory is already working to pass additional legislation to address this ongoing health and economic crisis. We'll be in touch with more updates soon. Booker HQ _P.S. We know this is a difficult time for many folks. If you 'd like to receive fewer emails during this public health crisis, **[please click here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0f88/5fca0d19/7a77e544/291d1635/1879893805/VEsH/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=1+-+please+click+here&utm_campaign=em200403&source=em200403")**._

---

---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Amanda?=
subject: Roundtable Discussion: Where is Pinellas County As It Relates To COVID-19?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda Makki For Congress Inc
body:
| | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/amandamakki/amanda-makki-for-congress-update-1472685?e=bbeef49760)
---
| | |
---
| | |
#
**_Roundtable Discussion:_**

**_Where is Pinellas County as it Relates To COVID-19?_**
**Sunday, April 5th at 2:00 p.m.**

---
|
---
**Amanda Makki**
Republican Candidate
Florida’s 13th District
| | | |
---
**Rick Baker**
Former St. Petersburg, FL Mayor
(2001-2010)
| |
---
**Dr. Sunit Srivastava, MD **
Board-Certified Internal Medicine Specialist, Founder, Legacy Health Medical Group, LLC & Chief of Medicine at Largo Medical Center
| | |
**Join Amanda Makki, Republican Candidate for Florida’s 13th Congressional District this Sunday, April 5th at 2:00 p.m. as she moderates a roundtable discussion on COVID-19 in Pinellas County. **

There’s a lot of national news about COVID-19, but what's happening in Pinellas County? We will hear from Congressional Candidate for Florida’s 13th District, Amanda Makki, Former St. Petersburg Mayor Rick Baker and Dr. Sunit Srivastava Board Certified Internist focusing on Geriatric Care who has been caring for patients in Pinellas County’s Hospitals, Rehabilitation and Assisted Living Facilities, and can speak to our successes in Pinellas County!

**_About Dr. Sunit Srivastava_**
Dr. Sunit Srivastava is a Board-Certified Internal Medicine Specialist. He did his training at Wright State University Boonshoft School of Medicine, University of South Florida (USF in Tampa), Tampa General Hospital and Moffit Cancer Center.

He is currently the Chief of Medicine at Largo Medical Center, the Director of the Hospital Medicine Program and the Caring Connection Program at the Palms of Pasadena Hospital, and the founder of Legacy Health Medical Group, LLC in Largo, FL.

His interests are Boating, fishing, running, weight lifting, scuba diving, traveling, gardening, and much more.

_**About Rick Baker**_
Rick Baker has been a Business Acquisition lawyer, a Law Firm President, a University Vice President and President of a St. Petersburg-based development and entertainment company. Presently he is an advisor and investor in companies involved in urban development and autonomous vehicle technology.

>From 2001 to 2010, he served as mayor of St. Petersburg - Florida’s fourth largest city. Under Mayor Baker’s leadership St. Petersburg experienced a renaissance unmatched in city history. In 2008, Mayor Baker was named America’s Mayor/Public Official of the Year by _Governing Magazine_ , and in 2011, was named “America’s greatest mayor of the decade” in _Newsweek Magazine’s Daily Beast._

Baker chaired the _Century Commission for a Sustainable Florida_ , Chaired the _Florida Statewide Water Congress_ , chaired the _Florida Governor’s Municipal Mentoring Initiative_ , vice chaired the _Florida Governor’s Energy Action_ _Team_ , and led in transition teams for the Florida Governor, Attorney General and Chief Financial Officer.

Baker holds a B.S., MBA, and a Juris Doctor (with honors) from Florida State University where he was Senior Class President. He studied comparative law at Oxford University. He served as legal intern to Florida Supreme Court Justice Ben Overton, and law clerk to Governor Bob Graham’s special counsel.

Baker is an adjunct fellow of the Manhattan Institute policy think-tank in New York City. He has authored three books – _Mangroves to Major League_ in 2000, a history of St. Petersburg; _The Seamless City_ in 2011, about city leadership and urban revitalization; and _Beyond the Sunshine_ in 2018 – a timeline history of Florida.

He enjoys music, guitar, weight training, history, public policy and counts as one of his favorite experiences playing and singing with the Marshall Tucker Band on four occasions.

---
| [Register](https://amandamakki.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=532f133099f0522d682bab7e9&id=8310beca71&e=bbeef49760 "Register")

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Thank you.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
| **From the Desk of Jay Inslee** Friend, Mr. Rogers once told a story that many of us reach back to in these moments: _"When I was a boy and I would see scary things in the news, my mother would say to me, 'Look for the helpers. You will always find people who are helping.'"_ And I look around and see so many helpers: health care workers such as doctors, nurses, paramedics, and nursing assistants; grocery store employees; police officers; firefighters; janitors; and more -- helping and doing all they can to keep our communities safe. It's an incredible thing to behold and an incredible sacrifice. **Today, I just wanted to say: Thank you.** Whether you're a front-line worker yourself or a family member or friend who is supporting them from afar, you are helping us take care of each other in ways we don't even fully realize yet. When this is over -- and it will be over, someday soon -- you all will be the heroes the history books remember. Thank you for your sacrifice, and thank you for your service. Very truly yours, Jay
---

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: There can be no corruption or politics in this:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e74d/6cd27893/546e6e1b/1590617e/3644912501/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | When the Senate passed the $2 trillion coronavirus rescue bill, we made a promise. _Now we have to keep it._ I fought hard for protections for the middle class, for small businesses, for Main Street. For better unemployment benefits for people struggling during this difficult time. For expanded food stamp benefits. For our hospitals and those on the medical front lines. The last thing we need right now is a struggling family to be unable to buy food, a hospital unable to get medical equipment, a small business unable to get relief--all because politicians are getting in the way. **We have to make**** _absolutely sure_****that the money we've allocated goes**** _exactly where it should_****, that corruption or political influence doesn't delay critical aid, distribute it unfairly, or cause it to never show up.** That's where I'm _really_ going to get to work. **Through this coronavirus pandemic and beyond, I will be your watchdog. I will stand up to Donald Trump, his allies,****_anyone_****who looks to profit or misdirect relief during this crisis.** This isn't only about the Trump administration, either. This is about Mitch McConnell and his Republican allies in the U.S Senate. You'd better believe I'm going to be keeping an extra close watch on the Senate majority over the coming weeks and months, because now is _not_ the time for political games, extremist nonsense, or attempts to put Wall Street profits ahead of the health and safety of everyday Americans. This is a challenging time for our country. But this is also a time for all of us to come together, for government, community groups, businesses, and individuals to rally and fight for our future. We can do this together. _Jeff_ Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e74d/6cd27893/546e6e1b/1590617f/3644912501/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2020-04-03-promise&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e74d/6cd27893/546e6e1b/1590617c/3644912501/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2020-04-03-promise&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e74d/6cd27893/546e6e1b/1590617d/3644912501/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2020-04-03-promise&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2e74d/6cd27893/546e6e1b/1590617a/3644912501/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2020-04-03-promise&refcode2=express-buttons")

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: #INThisTogether
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
Governor Holcomb continues to take action to lead our state during this challenging time – both to help limit the spread of the virus, and to assist.. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/inthistogether?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_In This Together_** In a normal year, we'd now be one month away from Indiana's primary election (_now moved to June 2_). Campaign events and Lincoln Days would be in high-gear, and we'd be well into the cycle of presidential politics. But it's safe to say that 2020 isn't a normal year.

Instead of coming together for Lincoln Days, we have examples across the state of Republicans coming together in service of their fellow Hoosiers. It's stories like:
* Madison's new mayor, **Mayor Bob Courtney** , [hiring city residents who are out of work](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4a2c3ee072&e=1acd9bb3d6) for temporary city jobs.
* **Evansville Mayor Lloyd Winnecke** and his colleagues in Southwest Indiana [starting a fund that's raising millions of dollars](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c476cf3136&e=1acd9bb3d6) to support essential service nonprofits in the region.
* Leaders like **Zionsville Town Council member Alex Choi**(an anesthesiologist), who is one of thousands of healthcare professionals across our state stepping up to fill new roles in this fight against the coronavirus.

**Governor Holcomb** said it perfectly earlier this week as he announced a new campaign, #INThisTogether, to bring awareness to the need to hunker down and social distance. He said, "The good news is we can fight back. We can slow the spread. We can flatten the curve.”

That's what these leaders -- and so many more -- are doing today. Together, we're fighting back. Thank you for dedication to keeping Hoosiers healthy, and let's remember, we're in this together!

\- Chairman Kyle Hupfer

**PHOTO:** **Chairman Hupfer** and the Indiana Republican State Committee had a virtual meeting this week, joining Hoosiers across the state who are #INThisTogether -- hunkering down, but not slowing down.
---
|
---
| | **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fa201885ad&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Keeping Hoosiers Safe: Coronavirus**_ **Governor Holcomb** continues to take action to lead our state during this challenging time – both to help limit the spread of the virus, and to assist Hoosiers who are facing new difficulties.

Today, he signed an executive order directing Hoosiers to hunker down at home for the next two weeks, as well as extending Indiana's public health emergency for another 30 days. Additionally, he shared news that **President Trump** has officially approved his request for a major disaster declaration for all of Indiana's 92 counties.

Here are a few more of this week’s actions:
* **Schools:**
* Schools that reached 160 instruction days will be considered to have completed a full academic year.
* Schools short of 160 instruction days are required to provide at least 20 additional days of remote learning between now and the end of the academic year.
* **Graduating Seniors:**
* All high school seniors on track to graduate before schools were closed on March 19 will be provided with the flexibility they need to earn an Indiana diploma.
* **Preparing for COVID-19 Surge:**
* Removing regulatory barriers to allow for better preparation for a surge in positive COVID-19 cases.
* Clarifies elective, non-urgent surgical procedures.
* Gives the State Health Commissioner the authority to open temporary hospitals, if needed.
* **Working to Double ICU Bed Space and Ventilators:**
* Hospitals have a baseline of 1,432 ICU beds. Today, the capacity has increased to 1,940 (a 35% increase), with more coming online daily. Goal is to double ICU space.
* The baseline number of ventilators is 1,777 with the goal to double that as well.
* Hospitals are opening more space by ending elective procedures, consolidating operations between hospitals, reopening recently-closed buildings and reusing space within each unit.
* **Allowing More Medical Professionals to Step Forward:**
* Many medical professionals who do not currently hold an active license in Indiana may join the fight supporting our frontline medical staff, including:
* Medical professionals who retired or became inactive in the last five years
* Medical professionals who hold licenses in other states
* Medical professionals who held licenses in other states and retired or became inactive in the last five years
* Certain medical students and graduates
* As of earlier this week, more than 11,000 Hoosiers volunteers had stepped forward.

To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make you're following**Governor Holcomb** on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=93d37480f2&e=1acd9bb3d6), [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=da0b9e65b9&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [Instagram](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=44b56ef027&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

This week we learned that Indiana is testing 10 times as many Hoosiers as we were just a few weeks ago. As new resources are coming online, that that number is only continuing to increase.

Thank you to every Hoosier who is doing his or her part to stop the spread of the coronavirus. As **Governor Holcomb** said earlier this week, “Whatever you’re doing, you’re in the game and playing an MVP role.”
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=04c6342a00&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Resources: A State That Works Together**_
As news and information on the coronavirus continue to rapidly change -- we want to make sure you have the resources you need to stay up to date!
* **Indiana State Department of Health** \- The Indiana State Department of Health is posting important information, and frequent updates to their website -- as well as offering an Indiana coronavirus map, and other resources for Hoosiers! [Check out their website here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cfd973dadf&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
* **Indiana Coronavirus Hotline** \- Get help with any questions you have regarding COVID-19 via Indiana's toll-free, 24/7 hotline! **Call 877-826-0011**!
* **FAQ's on Indiana's Stay-at-Home Order** \-- This is definitely a new challenge for all of us. [Click here to help answer some of your questions](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ef84a85da5&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
* **File for Unemployment** \- Many Hoosiers are temporarily unemployed due to COVID-19. [Click here for information and to file for unemployment](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=957bc25735&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

We're in this together! Help one another, and share these resources with friends and family who need them!
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=31ad8e1159&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Senator Braun and Senator Young's Coronavirus Resources**_
Are you a small business owner or a Hoosier worker impacted by the coronavirus pandemic? **U.S. Senator Mike Braun** put together a helpful resource guide to help you access resources available during this difficult time. For small businesses, this includes information about payroll support, employee retention credits, unemployment insurance enhancements and more.

[Click here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6e81245763&e=1acd9bb3d6) to view **Senator Braun's** resource guide!

**U.S. Senator Todd Young** has also put together a toolkit for Hoosiers and businesses impacted. This includes information about support for individuals and families, information about how new federal legislation is bolstering our healthcare response, and some FAQs.

Click here to view **Senator Young's** [coronavirus toolkit](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3ee37fa431&e=1acd9bb3d6).

[\--> Click here for Senator Young's coronavirus toolkit for Hoosiers <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8f8ac93db8&e=1acd9bb3d6)

| **_Feeling Called To Help Your Indiana Republican Team?_** This week, your Indiana Republican Party hosted a virtual phone bank -- and it was a success! Volunteers from across the state reached out to Hoosier voters while still staying socially distant.

Making voter calls is a great way to pass time, and support **Governor Holcomb** and your Republican team, all while hunkering down at home.

If you're feeling called to help while staying at home, [sign up here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3d0d2894fd&e=1acd9bb3d6)! Campaign events and Lincoln Days would be in high-gear, and we'd be well into the cycle of presidential politics. But it's safe to say that 2020 isn't a normal year. Instead of coming together for Lincoln Days, we have examples across the state of Republicans coming together in service of their fellow Hoosiers. It's stories like: Madison's new mayor, Mayor Bob Courtney , for temporary city jobs. Evansville Mayor Lloyd Winnecke and his colleagues in Southwest Indiana to support essential service nonprofits in the region. Leaders like Zionsville Town Council member Alex Choi(an anesthesiologist), who is one of thousands of healthcare professionals across our state stepping up to fill new roles in this fight against the coronavirus. Governor Holcomb said it perfectly earlier this week as he announced a new campaign, #INThisTogether, to bring awareness to the need to hunker down and social distance. He said, "The good news is we can fight back. We can slow the spread. We can flatten the curve.” That's what these leaders -- and so many more -- are doing today. Together, we're fighting back. Thank you for dedication to keeping Hoosiers healthy, and let's remember, we're in this together! \- Chairman Kyle Hupfer PHOTO: Chairman Hupfer and the Indiana Republican State Committee had a virtual meeting this week, joining Hoosiers across the state who are #INThisTogether -- hunkering down, but not slowing down. | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Keeping Hoosiers Safe: Coronavirus Governor Holcomb continues to take action to lead our state during this challenging time – both to help limit the spread of the virus, and to assist Hoosiers who are facing new difficulties. Today, he signed an executive order directing Hoosiers to hunker down at home for the next two weeks, as well as extending Indiana's public health emergency for another 30 days. Additionally, he shared news that President Trump has officially approved his request for a major disaster declaration for all of Indiana's 92 counties. Here are a few more of this week’s actions: Schools: Schools that reached 160 instruction days will be considered to have completed a full academic year. Schools short of 160 instruction days are required to provide at least 20 additional days of remote learning between now and the end of the academic year. Graduating Seniors: All high school seniors on track to graduate before schools were closed on March 19 will be provided with the flexibility they need to earn an Indiana diploma. subject: Small business relief is now up and running!
body:
President Trump signed the historic CARES Act into law just hours after Congress passed it last Friday. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2667f31792&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 3, 2020
---
| | |
# **Small business relief is now up and running!**

**President Trump** signed the historic CARES Act into law just hours after Congress passed it last Friday. One of the major wins from that deal is a new program to help America’s small businesses weather this global pandemic.

**🎬** [President Trump is putting small businesses FIRST!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=38eec76db5&e=3ee4985eea)

**The Paycheck Protection Program** is now up and running, offering nearly $350 billion in forgivable loans to keep companies operational and their workers employed. If eligible businesses and organizations retain their full staff and payroll—and use their loan proceeds on payroll or other eligible expenses—their loan will be 100% forgiven.

**“Small Business** **is the backbone** of the American economy, and the President has put the nation’s 30 million small businesses front and center in the response effort,” Small Business Administrator Jovita Carranza said yesterday. “And we are working hard to get them money quickly.”

[Learn more about Paycheck Protection Program loans here.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7bcb6cf9e4&e=3ee4985eea)

**Defeating****this Coronavirus** will require the combined effort of every American. If we all take care to follow the President’s [Updated Coronavirus Guidelines](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ac321e7f65&e=3ee4985eea), which include instructions for social distancing, we will save many American lives in the weeks ahead.

**Small businesses** are shouldering a large part of that burden, and President Trump wants to make sure they never face this challenge alone.

**“This administration believes** whole heartedly that if you are a small business, you are a critical part of the economic fabric of this country. And your viability is critical to the economic well-being of your employees,” Carranza said.

[Trump Admin Pushes Out Hundreds of Millions in Small Business Loans on First Day](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb14f9c253&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎬****WATCH:** [We’re fighting to keep small businesses open and employees on payroll](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=deadc79deb&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| [**First Lady Melania Trump shares photo of the White House lit blue in honor of World Autism Awareness Day**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2e57529e27&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2fdfb80914&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e6a38c50ba&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=401a8648f9)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

name: MJ Hegar
subject: Runoff Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
My campaign has been working hard to adapt to our new reality, and I wanted to take a moment to share some highlights from the past few weeks.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903af/7f51f7bc/4cf35eb3/4ef1cfd8/1329071836/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter, I know it's been a really tough month for many of us. I'm feeling incredibly lucky that my family is healthy and safe right now, and I'm so grateful to those on the frontlines making that possible. I'm also finding silver linings in getting to spend more time with my kids.

My campaign has been working hard to adapt to our new reality, and I wanted to take a moment to share some highlights from the past few weeks:

[**Your donation right now is critical to supporting our work and allowing the campaign to continue pushing our digital presence. Please pitch in if you can!**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903af/7f51f7bc/4cf35eb3/4ef1cfdc/1329071836/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExNjcifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200403.2nd__1167__&amount=10&recurring=true") | **We recently held our first virtual Q &A with the Dallas County East Democrats - I'm looking forward to more digital events in the coming weeks!**
---
| [](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903af/7f51f7bc/4cf35eb3/4ef1cfdc/1329071836/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExNjcifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200403.2nd__1167__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---

| [](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903af/7f51f7bc/4cf35eb3/4ef1cfdc/1329071836/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExNjcifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200403.2nd__1167__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---
**We also just launched our Digital Organizing Bootcamp to get our volunteers the tools and training they need to get our message out there.

Our text banking and phone banking volunteers have been spreading our message across Texas, all without leaving their houses!**

**And one of the updates I 'm most proud of from the last month is that because of my commitment to defending reproductive rights, I earned the endorsement of both EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Action Fund.**
---
| [](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903af/7f51f7bc/4cf35eb3/4ef1cfdc/1329071836/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExNjcifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200403.2nd__1167__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---

But through it all -- and most importantly -- I got to spend time with my family. My mother came to stay with us through isolation, and my kids were so excited to have her here!
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903af/7f51f7bc/4cf35eb3/4ef1cfdc/1329071836/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExNjcifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200403.2nd__1167__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---
We came through this last month strong, all thanks to our grassroots team. Right now, I'm counting on all of our grassroots supporters to help out as we push on through.

I'm feeling incredibly lucky that my family is healthy and safe right now, and I'm so grateful to those on the frontlines making that possible. I'm also finding silver linings in getting to spend more time with my kids. My campaign has been working hard to adapt to our new reality, and I wanted to take a moment to share some highlights from the past few weeks: "" | We recently held our first virtual Q &A with the Dallas County East Democrats - I'm looking forward to more digital events in the coming weeks! | "" | "" We also just launched our Digital Organizing Bootcamp to get our volunteers the tools and training they need to get our message out there. Our text banking and phone banking volunteers have been spreading our message across Texas, all without leaving their houses! And one of the updates I 'm most proud of from the last month is that because of my commitment to defending reproductive rights, I earned the endorsement of both EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Action Fund. | "" But through it all -- and most importantly -- I got to spend time with my family. My mother came to stay with us through isolation, and my kids were so excited to have her here! "" We came through this last month strong, all thanks to our grassroots team. subject: How to vote safely during COVID-19
body:
The latest from the Outvote blog, plus a look back at When We All Vote's epic #CouchParty. | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/4043973f87e4/april-3-2020?e=0aad15100c)

|

| _Organizing for everyone._
---
| | | |

|

| [ ](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=a211209af5&e=0aad15100c)

COVID-19 is affecting almost every area of life, including the 2020 primary election schedule. Check out our latest blog post to make sure you're up to date on casting your vote this spring.
| [Read On](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=d34df3b5e7&e=0aad15100c "Read On")

_4.1.20 | The Atlantic_

Maintaining our connection to community while staying safe.

---
[**Across the Country, Campaign Operatives Are Stuck**](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=9f26c416a0&e=0aad15100c)
_3.31.20 | POLITICO_

Organizers pushing forward despite difficult circumstances.
---
|
---
|
---
|
**Highlight of the Week: **

---
| |
---
On March 25, Michelle Obama's [When We All Vote](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=693b37992d&e=0aad15100c) held its inaugural #CouchParty. Thousands of enthusiastic organizers from around the country came together for an evening of registering voters on Outvote - and we reached almost **500,000 voters**! [Follow When We All Vote on Instagram](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=5eda5c603d&e=0aad15100c) to be the first to hear about our next #CouchParty.

|

**\- Mr. Rodgers**

name: Tina Smith
subject: Internet access is more important than ever
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Team, This week, I made a bipartisan push to help small broadband providers deliver discounted or free internet service to low-income families and those in rural areas during this time. Internet access isn’t just nice to have during this pandemic — it’s necessary — to support telehealth, student learning and jobs. That’s why I helped introduce the _Keeping Critical Connections Act._ Students who are finishing up their school year at home need to be able to connect to online classes. Employees who are working from home are counting on broadband to help them do their jobs. And folks are relying on the internet to help them access care through telehealth. I’m glad to work in a bipartisan way to help Minnesotans stay connected during this time. But I want to know my grassroots supporters are with me on this. **[I need 10,000 grassroots supporters (like you!) to demand internet access for all communities and send a powerful message back to Washington. Will you add your name to my petition before midnight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16859919/179474752/-285770906?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYjc0MTRjNy0xMzc2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UlbJzej5UpXXoAJp7aQ0HeKdFVVZALQcRFS9h-3wtik=&emci=c98ed5bf-1376-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=1b7414c7-1376-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=6227932)** | [Add your name](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16859920/179474753/-285770906?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYjc0MTRjNy0xMzc2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UlbJzej5UpXXoAJp7aQ0HeKdFVVZALQcRFS9h-3wtik=&emci=c98ed5bf-1376-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=1b7414c7-1376-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=6227932)
---
We must fight so that all communities have the resources they need to get through this crisis.
Thank you so much for your help. It means so much to me.
— Tina

name: Team Warner
subject: protecting ALL Virginians
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
We want to bring you updates on what Mark Warner is working on to make sure that all Virginians are protected during this difficult time.
|
---
| | | If you would like more information about how to slow and stop the spread of COVID-19, **[take a look at these resources from the Centers for Disease Control (CDC).](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899ca8/60520769/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html")**
---
Derek --

We want to bring you updates on what Mark Warner is working on to make sure that all Virginians are protected during this difficult time.

Here are just some of Mark's priorities for this week:
* Mark and many of his Senate colleagues called on the Treasury and Social Security Administration to make sure Social Security recipients could receive their direct stimulus payment without having to file a tax return. **Shortly thereafter, the Trump administration[reversed its decision](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899c97/60520769/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/social-security-recipients-relief-payments-directly-bank-account/story?id=69938029").**

* Mark called for the **[protection of federal contractors](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899c96/60520769/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://augustafreepress.com/warner-urges-omb-to-clarify-relief-for-national-security-contractors/")** during the coronavirus outbreak and clarification of what the CARES act provides for those working on critical national security missions.

* Mark continued his work **[to make sure all students have the internet access](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899c95/60520769/VEsC/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://edscoop.com/senators-ask-emergency-funding-ensure-students-have-internet-access/")** they need to continue learning during this outbreak, so that no child's education is disrupted due to social distancing directives.

* Mark spoke out about the coronavirus outbreak aboard the U.S.S. Theodore Roosevelt and urged the Navy to do more to keep our sailors safe from coronavirus.

Before we sign off, we have an update on our fundraiser for the Federation of Virginia Food Banks from last week. Thanks to your generosity, **we raised over $11,000 to support this important organization during a really critical time.**

From all of us at Team Warner: **Thank you from the bottom of our hearts.** We're truly blown away by your willingness to support your community during this difficult and uncertain time.

If you weren't able to give last week and would like to contribute now, you can use the link below. All donations collected go directly to the Federation of Virginia Food Banks, and are used to provide food to people across the Commonwealth.
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899c94/60520769/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjUzMiJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-covidfoodbank?refcode=20200403_f_1_fl__532__&refcode2=cont&amount=5")**
We're facing a difficult time, but we will get through it together. Today (and every day), we on Team Warner are grateful for this network of supporters.

Be well,

Team Warner

|
---

[](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899c93/60520769/VEsA/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://markwarnerva.com") Friends of Mark Warner
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213
Williamsburg, VA 23185

[Unsubscribe](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d530/53d9897e/54dcd8fa/1c899c92/60520769/VEsB/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/unsubscribe")

| | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
---

Mark spoke out about the coronavirus outbreak aboard the U.S.S. Theodore Roosevelt and urged the Navy to do more to keep our sailors safe from coronavirus. Before we sign off, we have an update on our fundraiser for the Federation of Virginia Food Banks from last week. Thanks to your generosity, we raised over $11,000 to support this important organization during a really critical time. From all of us at Team Warner: Thank you from the bottom of our hearts. We're truly blown away by your willingness to support your community during this difficult and uncertain time. If you weren't able to give last week and would like to contribute now, you can use the link below. All donations collected go directly to the Federation of Virginia Food Banks, and are used to provide food to people across the Commonwealth. "" We're facing a difficult time, but we will get through it together. Today (and every day), we on Team Warner are grateful for this network of supporters. Be well, Team Warner | "" Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 "" | | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

name: Tom Cotton
subject: URGENT: Tell the media to stop spouting Chinese propaganda
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate
body:
We cannot allow the mainstream media to continue to spread Chinese propaganda. The fact of the matter is that this global pandemic was made worse by the actions of the Chinese Communist party and their coverup.
Click here to [unsubscribe](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42179614&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MDMzNzUwNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

Peter,

Since January, I have pointed out two indisputable facts:

1\. Because of China's lies, we don't know where the China virus originated.

2\. The Wuhan Institute of Virology -- located near where the virus was first observed -- studies bat coronaviruses and has a history of unsafe practices.

**Therefore, we shouldn 't trust China's word and should investigate the origins of the China virus. **

For being willing to speak the truth about the Chinese Communist Party, I have been under constant attack. Check out these so-called reporters attacks on twitter.

[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=19&ms=NDIxNzk2MTQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MDMzNzUwNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

Now, the Washington Post, the Daily Caller, and numerous experts have confirmed that it is plausible that the China virus leaked from the Wuhan Institute of Virology.

The fact is, **[this was obvious from the beginning](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=NDIxNzk2MTQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MDMzNzUwNAS2&mt=1&rt=0).**

But instead of admitting that, the mainstream media and the left decided to spout Chinese Propaganda and attack me.

This is wrong. [**If you agree with me, will you sign this petition to call on the media to start questioning China 's lies?**](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=NDIxNzk2MTQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MDMzNzUwNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

I refuse to back down until we find out the truth and hold China accountable for its actions.

We cannot allow the mainstream media to continue to spread Chinese propaganda. The fact of the matter is that this global pandemic was made worse by the actions of the Chinese Communist party and their coverup.

**[SIGN THE PETITION: CHINA IS LYING: STOP TRUSTING THEM](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=NDIxNzk2MTQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MDMzNzUwNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Indiana's education updates
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Governor Holcomb reminded Hoosiers that COVID-19 doesn’t distinguish between groups of people. Every single one of us is a potential vessel that could | | |
---|---|---
Friend,

_**“I want to stress how important it is that we’re each taking care of ourselves and one another. Because we’re all in this together...and we’re going to get through this together.” –Governor Eric Holcomb.**_

A few big updates today: Governor Holcomb _**extended Indiana’s stay-at-home order for two weeks (through April 20).** _Originally set to expire Monday night, this means that we’ll be hunkering down at home a little longer – all with the critical aim of saving Hoosier lives.

Governor Holcomb also _**extended Indiana’s public health emergency declaration for another 30 days,** _now expiring on May 3.

And a word of thanks – President Trump has approved the governor’s health emergency declaration request for all of Indiana’s 92 counties.

_**"We have to stay physically distant, but socially connected and there are some many example of Hoosiers connecting, sharing moments of joy and solidarity, while still being physically distant.”**_

These aren’t normal times and it is understandable that many Hoosiers are experiencing feelings of uncertainty, nervousness and increased anxiety. The importance of caring for your own mental health can’t be overstated. If you’re feeling higher levels of stress and uncertainty, watch this video from Governor Holcomb’s press conference today to learn some strategies that might help:
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=5ffd9deb209b410d9e6422392384c837&_e=csx2880K8agk1zhgbJ10u7CW5njWK7PFk71rU0mhQt7xL-qw6akfpwZJD3so8nxvUTFZXX8J_tYXJcLKO1RAu8Owu3vv-bJxBZOfKV-cPlih0AznwL45-BeGry9HwU0r7DGAmE3CdjSZhdSPc-QavR-BNNhsfxNK09NIS6pkNe4-iwhRQ11f2bBEPIL-wzNHmOi9lb0sxBqExqx96yuxbQldRfttWEfu3GYdtjIqzi9164Xk3zgLIKEeVuHeBPfVID4naY9fZ70yOmiuGJeutfVDL0T1HFBpw-8cVemUTzHO3-ECu5fsjQrDO0aVwn_gqeSkBp74ShtYQKnpbPROwwwM7KwhCSOw7JQG1w5UwW3hRzZtymyPd707kx5xkX1NglxgR60s_NSrWkjtLMYa9U0BpP_o3M2ITYo_oRkQD4sMUIwGNBEvTxv8CXorZVhHJLsBuzVA-jXFJ0arRLANEw%3D%3D)
[ **Learn Strategies to Support Your Mental Health** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=5ffd9deb209b410d9e6422392384c837&_e=csx2880K8agk1zhgbJ10u7CW5njWK7PFk71rU0mhQt7xL-qw6akfpwZJD3so8nxvUTFZXX8J_tYXJcLKO1RAu8Owu3vv-bJxBZOfKV-cPlih0AznwL45-BeGry9HwU0r7DGAmE3CdjSZhdSPc-QavR-BNNhsfxNK09NIS6pkNe4-iwhRQ11f2bBEPIL-wzNHmOi9lb0sxBqExqx96yuxbQldRfttWEfu3GYdtjIqzi9164Xk3zgLIKEeVuHeBPfVsFaogQDys6EGo0TSQaCgnZhZSBo4_uVHAZII-6ye-knc6GFRqtWUMxDjzCwbTBLdOzRpha9loaHd9CvZaAhryVcKWQ5e2bpSfNFA1B1DJATZ1bxxZd2csoNlIGILxkSClSYMnzdbPxGxMQaro0Y8I-tLtNnkMINNNgfGjYsOOMiy2bRT_TMlfHddf8Zwoot0GY6epvpUy1oFehEXn7mWSg%3D%3D)
Here are a few tips that might be helpful during this time:
* Take a walk outside (while practicing social distancing).
* Read books.
* Write a list of what’s bothering you and circle what you can control.
* Seek counseling through telemedicine help.
* Check with your primary care doctor about possible medicinal treatments.
* Call 2-1-1 to connect with additional resources.

As we sail through this storm together, we want folks to know that there is help available. _**It is OK to seek help for your mental health and there is help and support available for you!** _

Additionally, yesterday Governor Holcomb _**moved all Indiana schools to remote instruction for the remainder of the 2019-2020 academic year.**_ The Indiana Department of Education provided further clarification on graduation requirements for seniors. A school corporation may issue an Indiana diploma to a student who has done all of the following:
* Has met all of the course and credit requirements for the specific diploma designation based on a combination of high school credits earned prior to and the course in which a student was enrolled as of March 19, when the governor issued the statewide school closure.
* Meets any virtual or remote learning participation requirements established by the governing body of the local school corporation in response to the statewide school closure order issued by the governor.
* Meets any additional graduation requirements established by the governing body of the local school corporation prior to the school closure order issued by the governor.

**[Complete flexibility from Indiana graduation requirements in response to COVID-19 can be found here.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=5ffd9deb209b410d9e6422392384c837&_e=csx2880K8agk1zhgbJ10u_CXXOi49-ofHLXVn3l84Five2LYuWPrUJ2bSaa4JjOa0V5UVloGe3f1p1y-DdPnKi_6yI2DidGzBOLrZKSkrCDqeyJWuFEtr96hOExj6PfYgv0vVpwvNn-0lEh62oJJczk-ldGanaK1C4tC_KDw6IYdMZ7EBG_vdQewfZ85g-NHOFUDlOXy-Nkify0OvjXl872uMn4mJkcBF_QoMnFu1LXVlujjqZuhG2is6HZ18MVCV_pLvZw8w8KxmDeTVuh6xARcCBEogvEhVxnEcLVGfFDyHw5r1VFApXsofBZ4TvFWx4pNtI5SZUi4l5I0V-0jVVyVsC9zVRWA4LJuzMpHnjPe2jWo-ngRaH90VLQulWX7OtiCWgeyXNToiKoelWx6oUXD8S3tz5VyHjZBRPY45oTHkAnx0C166fAKgOQQQRbl0fUso98b-lolai50Bz3yFw%3D%3D)**

The best thing we can do to protect our loved ones is follow these guidelines and flatten the curve as quickly as possible.

_**“There is going to be reconstruction and there is going to be rebuilding and we’re going to do it together.” –Governor Holcomb.**_

#INThisTogether,
-Your Holcomb Crew
---
---
|
|
| |
---

|

name: Joe Biden 2020
subject: re: our latest survey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
We're coming to you with our latest policy priorities survey. We don't have a response from you yet -- and we would greatly appreciate your input. |
---
Peter --

The Joe Biden campaign is people-powered. We operate at our strongest when all of our supporters weigh in on the top issues of the day, and share their concerns about the direction of our country.

**That 's why we're coming to you with our latest policy priorities survey. We don't have a response from you yet -- and we would greatly appreciate your input.**

[**Can you fill out the survey here? It should only take a minute of your time »**](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d9/1459795427/VEsH/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "http://go.joebiden.com/page/s/take-our-2020-policy-priorities-survey?source=JB_EM_SURV_2020.03.28_B1_priorities_X__F1_S2_C1__ns_reac-tier2-send11_t2&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_SURV_2020.03.28_B1_priorities_X__F1_S2_C1__ns_reac-tier2-send11_t2") | _**2020 POLICY PRIORITIES SURVEY**_
**Name:** Peter Willis **Survey Completion: **Not Yet!

Your fellow supporters have responded that right now they are most concerned about providing masks for health care workers, making tests readily available, and ensuring food for all families.

But, we'd be remiss to close this survey without hearing from you first, Peter.

**So can you click here and add your answers today? We 're really counting on your response.**
[TAKE SURVEY >>](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d8/1459795427/VEsF/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/take-our-2020-policy-priorities-survey?source=JB_EM_SURV_2020.03.28_B1_priorities_X__F1_S2_C1__ns_reac-tier2-send11_t2&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_SURV_2020.03.28_B1_priorities_X__F1_S2_C1__ns_reac-tier2-send11_t2")

Team Joe
| | [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d7/1459795427/VEsC/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://www.facebook.com/joebiden") [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d6/1459795427/VEsD/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://twitter.com/joebiden") [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d5/1459795427/VEsA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://www.youtube.com/joebiden") [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d4/1459795427/VEsB/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://www.instagram.com/joebiden")
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If we got your name or any information wrong -- we're so sorry! Peter, thank you so much for supporting Joe Biden's Presidential campaign.

[ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d0/1459795427/VEsHBA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer&refcode=email-bsd-20190515host-b20_FDR_states_NA-F1_S1_C1")

This isn't the time to be complacent. If you are ready to fight for the soul of this nation, you can start by donating to elect Joe Biden by clicking the button below.

We know we send a lot of emails, and we are sorry about that. The reason? We are relying on grassroots supporters like you (we're serious!).

But Peter, we don't want to bother you. If you'd like to only receive our most important emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29318699/1cd0ac28/b9d49e08/357899d2/1459795427/VEsHBg/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-emails"). A few big updates today: Governor Holcomb _**extended Indiana’s stay-at-home order for two weeks (through April 20).** _Originally set to expire Monday night, this means that we’ll be hunkering down at home a little longer – all with the critical aim of saving Hoosier lives.

Governor Holcomb also _**extended Indiana’s public health emergency declaration for another 30 days,** _now expiring on May 3.

And a word of thanks – President Trump has approved the governor’s health emergency declaration request for all of Indiana’s 92 counties.

_**"We have to stay physically distant, but socially connected and there are some many example of Hoosiers connecting, sharing moments of joy and solidarity, while still being physically distant.”**_

These aren’t normal times and it is understandable that many Hoosiers are experiencing feelings of uncertainty, nervousness and increased anxiety. The importance of caring for your own mental health can’t be overstated. If you’re feeling higher levels of stress and uncertainty, watch this video from Governor Holcomb’s press conference today to learn some strategies that might help:
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3c5e856d2c714625b4ae370956417df2&_e=ytr03x8wXmuX1-di2-TsZDKoqp4kdYKoemSNqDmmajEBcIWcNW50nvGFjeDvCFzUqAn4XegcvMmiKTODdtL8hPOcta9Meq8pFLKneKrWvGKHFfz75vWAmoYXmy8im8s32fIKtpBE4xjFHT5T4QRMO4gdhEecww2Kk0fx3idSKRlVjD1BAeCy3CDx7HUOaqqGIDIgp5DS__tEZIpjUSYv1SjtDEWP77_bO61ySzFgkcdDwj6dy75UdScDfc_d4rMeyFLlRSx2x8eapheVUHnJriweK7duD5He4yqU1BOTUJLEEzuqAbDUNUjFh3wcuUdtA4IXGVQa2qhBJUGDdw_MnthCdPpLazDLYWMkujKyjho6Gvo5LaFbtgcF9yX2SvjoqPW1RR8ikhVPyqLm2XQceGbLI560DtPYWJsnkz6Lzkugy8xUli9Uf98yoR548ljPtSeOVriYGDuBI64wJ1moiA%3D%3D)
[ **Learn Strategies to Support Your Mental Health** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3c5e856d2c714625b4ae370956417df2&_e=ytr03x8wXmuX1-di2-TsZDKoqp4kdYKoemSNqDmmajEBcIWcNW50nvGFjeDvCFzUqAn4XegcvMmiKTODdtL8hPOcta9Meq8pFLKneKrWvGKHFfz75vWAmoYXmy8im8s32fIKtpBE4xjFHT5T4QRMO4gdhEecww2Kk0fx3idSKRlVjD1BAeCy3CDx7HUOaqqGIDIgp5DS__tEZIpjUSYv1SjtDEWP77_bO61ySzFgkcdDwj6dy75UdScDfc_d4rMeslBalg45h4YNVaOo8LaK08MkSnw0HqjYt_KFSyIC6jqzEIUeXnLQ33Y27tnBrfd7r4ti5mkT3zSdB8AOVO0PDwsaBTIOgetzKpRtTYPvnFufWTAdQYZRuDAu_G5SG8_0puhpPgRTkDdqcWetH_F-kdIuKaEYnXsP8a48Z8usKOnoCGnR_Fzr3U9afhXeNuS8bYwbs051PBNSthMnuclMSA%3D%3D)
Here are a few tips that might be helpful during this time:
* Take a walk outside (while practicing social distancing).
* Read books.
* Write a list of what’s bothering you and circle what you can control.
* Seek counseling through telemedicine help.
* Check with your primary care doctor about possible medicinal treatments.
* Call 2-1-1 to connect with additional resources.

As we sail through this storm together, we want folks to know that there is help available. _**It is OK to seek help for your mental health and there is help and support available for you!** _

Additionally, yesterday Governor Holcomb _**moved all Indiana schools to remote instruction for the remainder of the 2019-2020 academic year.**_ The Indiana Department of Education provided further clarification on graduation requirements for seniors. A school corporation may issue an Indiana diploma to a student who has done all of the following:
* Has met all of the course and credit requirements for the specific diploma designation based on a combination of high school credits earned prior to and the course in which a student was enrolled as of March 19, when the governor issued the statewide school closure.
* Meets any virtual or remote learning participation requirements established by the governing body of the local school corporation in response to the statewide school closure order issued by the governor.
* Meets any additional graduation requirements established by the governing body of the local school corporation prior to the school closure order issued by the governor.

**[Complete flexibility from Indiana graduation requirements in response to COVID-19 can be found here.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3c5e856d2c714625b4ae370956417df2&_e=ytr03x8wXmuX1-di2-TsZPux_fp4orU9pWjdHBkpQG8G14slVJi1Oy2e5ExTokGXB7BA75Q3BAtsDUmjpFnNxtMtLuN93xXydK-70KQN1ldzMMNndolcPy5xVHQR6QncJ_36TW1lQPQiEdz0KXYBR7ROtopyzGEboaXiT_6XSP8x6fdpiBIOyGDkeRfZuKGJDJp2XABDBPOOt3Xz8pyn2r1NuGopRLwtx-3VyzSHdCrVcrhATfhuGmtEqwHd7aWwNFVEBuVKK51YUSKpW_uXCpaTI9rwncIVKbIV9zTQPEn167TnRIc_2b-4gccHNWvHg3SaN6ICBFSl6Lh-gkr3udOq3zlljw9mOwj_GPxAt36RyhWOavlAWerl8WkyuLIcO9ccxrsqKQIDXoB3-uND5geKsFmCbSlCIu9xoOI6zGvWmJvYC7RsLIOUvaczs5sRbzUSCalIYP79cOoSlMPGYQ%3D%3D)**

The best thing we can do to protect our loved ones is follow these guidelines and flatten the curve as quickly as possible.

_**“There is going to be reconstruction and there is going to be rebuilding and we’re going to do it together.” –Governor Holcomb.**_

#INThisTogether,
-Your Holcomb Crew

name: Team Christy
subject: Send a message on May 12th
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | _The number one priority during this time is to make sure everyone helps mitigate the spread of COVID-19 in California and across the country. Our campaign is doing our part by canceling all in-person events, moving to a digital operation, and supporting local efforts to help those in need. Emails like this are a critical part of our ability to run a strong campaign that can win._
Peter,

There's a lot of reasons our election is so special -- but one of the big ones is that it will be a referendum on Trump's handling of the COVID-19 crisis.

President Trump failed to take this crisis seriously early on, spread medical misinformation, coerced governors into praising him in return for medical relief to their states, and still has yet to get enough tests and protective gear into our communities -- just to start.

Mike Garcia will not only put up with this behavior in Congress, but he will welcome it. He even has pledged to vote with President Trump 100% of the time. **A win for Garcia is a message of approval for the way things have gone under Trump.**

That's not the message we want to send from the 25th District.

**[And it's why we need Christy in Congress. Help send a message on May 12th that we're fed up with Trump's failed leadership: Chip in $5 or more right now to help elect Christy.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8cf/29776869/a128e36/569fb435/2859097761/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA0MDNfdHJ1bXByZWZlcmVuZHVtLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2004_email_christysmith?refcode=em20_200403_trumpreferendum-full")** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [Express Donate $5](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8cf/29776869/a128e36/569fb436/2859097761/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA0MDNfdHJ1bXByZWZlcmVuZHVtLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2004_email_christysmith?refcode=em20_200403_trumpreferendum-full&express_lane=true&amount=5") | [Express Donate $25](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8cf/29776869/a128e36/569fb437/2859097761/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA0MDNfdHJ1bXByZWZlcmVuZHVtLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2004_email_christysmith?refcode=em20_200403_trumpreferendum-full&express_lane=true&amount=25")

[Express Donate $250](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8cf/29776869/a128e36/569fb432/2859097761/VEsA/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA0MDNfdHJ1bXByZWZlcmVuZHVtLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2004_email_christysmith?refcode=em20_200403_trumpreferendum-full&express_lane=true&amount=250") | [Donate Other Amount](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8cf/29776869/a128e36/569fb435/2859097761/VEsB/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA0MDNfdHJ1bXByZWZlcmVuZHVtLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2004_email_christysmith?refcode=em20_200403_trumpreferendum-full")
Right now, our district has no representation in Congress, but we have a choice in over a month for the type of leadership we want. **We think the choice is clear.**

Trump is playing partisan politics and focused on his own ego while people are dying. Mike Garcia is sending childish tweets from a shadow Twitter account.

Meanwhile, Christy is working with Republicans and Democrats in California to pass urgently needed relief for working families.

**This is the case we'll make to voters. But we can only do it with your support.**

**[So chip in $5 or more to Christy's campaign for Congress today -- we need leaders like her now more than ever.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8cf/29776869/a128e36/569fb435/2859097761/VEsO/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA0MDNfdHJ1bXByZWZlcmVuZHVtLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2004_email_christysmith?refcode=em20_200403_trumpreferendum-full")**

Thank you and stay safe.

\-- Team Christy
